summary: Well-scoped plan that introduces a uniform-guarded fallback for the size attribute across all four vertex shaders. The mix(1.0, size, uHasSizeAttr) pattern is robust and the changes are localized to the shader/geometry layer with no cross-cutting concerns.
strengths: The mix() guard is mathematically correct: mix(1.0, 0.0, 0.0)=1.0 handles WebGL's default 0.0 for unbound attributes
Uniform-based branching avoids shader recompilation or #ifdef complexity
Adding uHasSizeAttr to COMMON_UNIFORMS ensures consistency across all shader variants
Plan correctly identifies that crystalField bypasses shaderRegistry and handles it explicitly
No new architectural patterns introduced — extends the existing registry/validator model naturally
Test strategy covers GLSL source patterns, runtime uniform values, validator behavior, and registry structure

concerns.description: CrystalField currently bypasses shaderRegistry and geometryValidator entirely (inline uniforms, no validation call). The plan says to apply 'same changes as pointCloud.ts' but crystalField has a fundamentally different setup pattern.
concerns.recommendation: In Step 3, handle crystalField's inline uniform block separately rather than assuming registry-based setup. Add uHasSizeAttr directly to its manual uniforms object.

concerns.description: Step 4 updates geometryValidator to accept optional attrs, but crystalField never calls the validator. The optional attribute validation won't cover crystalField unless a validation call is also added.
concerns.recommendation: Consider adding a validation call in crystalField.ts as part of this ticket, or document it as a known gap.

concerns.description: The plan lists src/visual/types.ts as a file to modify but then says 'No structural changes needed'. This is a no-op entry.
concerns.recommendation: Remove from files_to_modify to avoid confusion.

dependency_analysis.dependency_satisfaction: US-049 (shader integrity) and US-050 (geometry validator) are both merged. The plan correctly builds on their registry structure and validation patterns.
dependency_analysis.downstream_impact: No downstream tickets are blocked by this change. Moving size to optional is additive — all systems still attach size, so existing behavior is preserved.

file_impact.hotspot_risk: shaderRegistry.ts is the highest-risk file as it affects all systems, but the change is additive (new arrays + one new uniform)

gaps: No test for crystalField's uHasSizeAttr since crystalField is excluded from existing shader integrity tests (T-049 tests only cover pointCloud, particleField, ribbonField)
No runtime WebGL compilation test — GLSL source pattern matching is a proxy but cannot catch all GPU-side issues

recommendations: Handle crystalField's divergent setup pattern explicitly in the implementation rather than treating it identically to the other three systems
Consider adding crystalField to the shader integrity test suite as part of this ticket since it touches that system
Remove the no-op types.ts entry from the plan
